tools.name: Bash
tools.description: Execute a bash command. Working directory: /workspace/group. Returns stdout + stderr.

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Read a file and return its contents with line numbers.

tools.name: Write
tools.description: Write content to a file (creates parent directories as needed).

tools.name: Edit
tools.description: Replace a specific string in a file. old_string must appear exactly once in the file.

old_string.description: Exact text to replace (must be unique in the file)

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern.

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Search file contents using ripgrep. Returns matching lines with file:line context.

flags.description: Extra rg flags e.g. "-i" (case insensitive), "-l" (filenames only)

tools.name: WebFetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL and return its text content (HTML stripped).

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a WhatsApp text message to the chat right now (before finishing). Use for progress updates.

tools.name: send_voice
tools.description: Send a WhatsApp voice note (spoken audio). Use for natural conversational replies, especially when the user sent a voice message. Keep text short — under ~200 words.

text.description: Text to speak aloud as a voice note

voice.description: Optional speaker: Ryan, Aiden, Vivian, Serena, Uncle_Fu, Dylan, Eric, Ono_Anna, Sohee (default: Ryan)

tools.name: send_image
tools.description: Send an image to the WhatsApp chat (e.g. a screenshot). Use when the user wants to see a page, diagram, or any image. Path must be under /workspace/group (e.g. page.png or /workspace/group/page.png).

path.description: Path to the image file, e.g. page.png or /workspace/group/page.png

tools.name: ask_boss
tools.description: Ask the user (the boss) for guidance when stuck, unsure, or before doing something risky/irreversible. Your question is sent to the chat. Stop and wait for their reply — do not proceed until you have it. Use when: ambiguous request, destructive action, sensitive data, or you lack context.

question.description: Clear question or context for the user to respond to

tools.name: schedule_task
tools.description: Schedule a recurring or one-time task that runs as an agent.

prompt.description: What the agent should do when the task runs

schedule_type.description: cron=time-based, interval=every N ms, once=run once

schedule_value.description: cron: "0 9 * * *" | interval: ms like "3600000" | once: local ISO "2026-02-01T15:30:00" (no Z!)

context_mode.description: group=with chat history, isolated=fresh session (include context in prompt)

tools.name: register_group
tools.description: Register a new WhatsApp group so the bot can respond there. Main group only. Use available_groups.json (in /workspace/ipc/) to find the JID. When the user says they added you to a group, call this to activate it.

requiresTrigger.description: If false, bot replies to all messages. Default true for groups.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: List all scheduled tasks.

tools.name: pause_task
tools.description: Pause a scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: resume_task
tools.description: Resume a paused scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: cancel_task
tools.description: Cancel and delete a scheduled task by ID.

tools.name: reset_session
tools.description: Clear this chat's conversation history so the next message starts fresh. Use when the user asks to reset the session, clear memory, start over, or forget the conversation.

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List all available workflows and automations in the registry. Call this when the user asks what you can do, what automations are available, or to browse capabilities.

tools.name: search_tools
tools.description: Search the workflow registry for tools/automations by keyword or intent. Returns matching entries with name, description, and how to run them. Call this first when the user asks you to do something that might be a pre-built workflow.

query.description: Keywords describing what you want to do, e.g. "morning briefing", "send slack message", "fetch leads"

tools.name: run_workflow
tools.description: Run a workflow by name from the registry. Optionally pass arguments as a JSON object.

args.description: Optional key-value arguments passed as environment variables to the script
